title: Blueberry Muffins
image_alt: Golden blueberry muffins with domed tops

variations.title: Triple Batch
items: Bake time stays the same — 18–20 minutes

items.name: milk or non-dairy milk, plus more as needed

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (204°C). Line a muffin tin with paper liners — 8 cups for large muffins, 10 for standard. Lightly grease or spray the top surface of the tin to prevent oversized tops from sticking.
Whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
In a measuring jug, add the oil, then crack in the egg. Pour in enough milk to bring the total liquid to the 1-cup line (about ⅓ cup milk). Add the vanilla and whisk until combined.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and mix gently until just combined — a few streaks of flour are fine. Fold in the blueberries. Do not overmix or the muffins will be tough.

Divide the batter evenly among the lined cups, filling each almost to the top. Bake for 18–20 minutes until the tops are golden and a toothpick inserted in the centre comes out clean. Cool in the pan for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.
